questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: An inorganic compound ' $\mathbf{X}$ ' of an alkali metal on heating gives a reddish-brown gas ' $\mathbf{Y}$ ' and a binary solid ' $\mathbf{Z}$ '. This solid is less soluble in water and its solution is basic. ' $\mathbf{X}$ ' does not give a positive silver nitrate test. ' $\mathbf{X}$ ' can be identified as

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
A sample of water from a river was analyzed for the presence of metal ions and the observations were recorded as given below
Reagent added Observation
dil. HCl No change
aq. $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ White precipitate
aq. $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ No change
The water sample is likely to contain

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Which of the following is correct?
I. Sodium (Na) is present as metal in nature
II. $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ is paramagnetic
III. $\mathrm{NaO}_2$ is paramagnetic
IV. Na reacts with $\mathrm{N}_2$ to form $\mathrm{Na}_3 \mathrm{~N}$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: The correct statement about the solubilities of Group 2 hydroxides is:
options: The solubilities increase because lattice energy increases as we go down Group 2
The solubilities increase because lattice energy decreases as we go down Group 2
The solubilities decrease because atomic size increases as we go down Group 2
The solubilities decrease because lattice energy decreases as we go down Group 2

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
A dilute solution of an alkali metal in liquid ammonia is
I. blue in colour
II. conducts electricity
III. paramagnetic
IV. an oxidising agent
